worked: **Convention-decision-table before bulk-editing** — when standardizing the 7 onboarding docs, I laid out an 8-row decision table (H1 prefix, blockquote, See-also, tags, trailing-line removal, etc.) with rationale per choice, then applied uniformly. Made the bulk edit auditable.

**Goal-blockquote-only stubs** — for the 6 scaffolds, writing ONLY the `> **Goal:**` line as prose + `TODO(human):` one-liners per section gives a doc that renders cleanly, signals intent, and is valid markdown — without fabricating content the user hasn't decided yet. Matches the user's "just titles + a goal" instruction exactly.

**Cross-link graph thinking** — designed the workflows hub as the center, with 3 onboarding entry points (router→hub, plan→specific docs, comms→slack-automation), so a new hire reaches engineering processes from whichever onboarding doc they enter. Verified all 64 links resolve.

**Whole-tree search before curating concept doc-lists** — searched the full `docs/` tree per concept rather than guessing paths; favored `_index`/`PRIMER`/`overview`/ADR docs over deep internals to match "high-level + introductory + referential".
didnt_work: **Read-tool display vs raw bytes (recurring)** — `cat` and the Read tool both rendered the `team/onboarding/_index.md` + `tech/workflows/_index.md` + `team/runbooks/_index.md` files with 2-space leading indents + line-wrapped absolute paths, making them LOOK gum-rendered/corrupted. Raw-byte inspection (`awk 'NR<=20 {printf "[%d:%d] %s", NR, length($0), $0}'`) confirmed the FILES ARE CLEAN — it's a display artifact, not file content. This is the SECOND time this fooled me this session. Rule reinforced: when a doc looks 2-space-indented via Read/cat, verify raw bytes before declaring it broken.

**Date drift across turns** — system reminders advanced the date 2026-05-18 → -19 → -20 → -24 across turns. New docs got `last_updated: 2026-05-19`; onboarding standardization got `2026-05-18`. Not wrong (reflects when each was touched) but a future audit might wonder why sibling docs differ by a day.
notes: The `tech/workflows/` folder is now the canonical engineering-process hub. Its `_index.md` has a "when to use which workflow" matrix. New process docs go here as `status: stub` first.

`tech/conventions/` = rules (reference, prescriptive); `tech/workflows/` = processes (procedural); `team/runbooks/` = reactive incident response. The hotfix doc lives in workflows (process defined ahead of time) but cross-links to runbooks (execution under pressure).
Onboarding H1 convention: indices/routers/plans keep "Onboarding" in the title; specific content docs (comms, project-set-up, team-intro) use bare titles.

The user makes intentional inline edits between turns (caught: Node version `22.11+`→`22.20+` in project-set-up.md; `Extractor`→`Extractor / Scraper`; added a Day-2 daily-sync bullet; `von@versable.ai` roster cleanup). Always re-read before editing; preserve their edits.
feedback: "Just files + structure + titles, write a goal per doc" — don't fabricate body content for scaffolds.
"Ignore the human todos" (from the standardization turn) — leave `[VERIFY]`/`TODO(human)` markers in place when restructuring.
The `scrape-success.modal.tsx` props-sprawl question was a WRONG PASTE — not a task.
Per memory `feedback_writing_is_user_interface.md` (added this session by user): no flattery openers, no unrequested option-menus, answer-and-stop.
